title: The Erie Canal opens
book: Bond of Union: Building the Erie Canal and the American Empire
author: Gerard Koeppel
note: A vivid narrative history of how the canal was conceived and dug, the engineering feat that made New York the nation's premier port.

title: The Commissioners' Plan grids Manhattan
book: Gotham: A History of New York City to 1898
author: Edwin G. Burrows and Mike Wallace
note: The Pulitzer Prize-winning definitive history of New York through 1898, authoritative on the grid plan and everything else in this era.

title: Croton water reaches Manhattan
book: Water for Gotham: A History
author: Gerard Koeppel
note: The standard history of New York's long, disaster-driven struggle for clean water, culminating in the Croton Aqueduct.

title: Famine Irish transform the city
book: Five Points: The 19th-Century New York City Neighborhood That Invented Tap Dance, Stole Elections, and Became the World's Most Notorious Slum
author: Tyler Anbinder
note: The acclaimed history of the immigrant district that received the Famine Irish, reconstructing Five Points from archives and archaeology.

title: Castle Garden becomes an immigrant depot
book: City of Dreams: The 400-Year Epic History of Immigrant New York
author: Tyler Anbinder
note: A sweeping narrative of New York as an immigrant city, tracing the arrival stations from Castle Garden to Ellis Island.

title: The state sets aside land for Central Park
book: The Park and the People: A History of Central Park
author: Roy Rosenzweig and Elizabeth Blackmar
note: The Historic Preservation Book Prize-winning social history of the park's making, including the displacement of Seneca Village.

title: The Greensward Plan wins Central Park
book: Genius of Place: The Life of Frederick Law Olmsted
author: Justin Martin
note: The leading modern biography of the Greensward Plan's co-designer and the father of American landscape architecture.

title: The Draft Riots convulse the city
book: The New York City Draft Riots: Their Significance for American Society and Politics in the Age of the Civil War
author: Iver Bernstein
note: The definitive scholarly account of the deadliest civil disturbance in American history and its political meaning.

title: The Tweed Ring is exposed
book: Boss Tweed: The Rise and Fall of the Corrupt Pol Who Conceived the Soul of Modern New York
author: Kenneth D. Ackerman
note: A brisk, well-documented narrative of Tammany's William Tweed, his machine and the newspaper campaign that brought him down.

title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art is founded
book: Gotham: A History of New York City to 1898
author: Edwin G. Burrows and Mike Wallace
note: The Pulitzer-winning master history of the Gilded Age city that produced the Met, the natural-history museum and its cultural institutions.

title: Five boroughs consolidate into Greater New York
book: Greater Gotham: A History of New York City from 1898 to 1919
author: Mike Wallace
note: The sequel to Gotham, opening with consolidation and the making of the five-borough metropolis; a monumental standard work.

title: Tenement House Act reforms housing
book: How the Other Half Lives: Studies Among the Tenements of New York
author: Jacob A. Riis
note: The landmark photojournalistic exposé of tenement squalor that drove the housing-reform movement behind the 1901 act.

title: First subway line opens (IRT)
book: 722 Miles: The Building of the Subways and How They Transformed New York
author: Clifton Hood
note: The standard history of how New York's subway was built and how it reshaped the city, starting with the 1904 IRT.

title: Ellis Island immigration hits its peak
book: City of Dreams: The 400-Year Epic History of Immigrant New York
author: Tyler Anbinder
note: A comprehensive narrative of immigrant New York that covers the Ellis Island peak years of southern and eastern European migration.

title: Triangle Shirtwaist Factory fire kills 146
book: Triangle: The Fire That Changed America
author: David von Drehle
note: The definitive popular history of the fire, the young immigrant women who died and the labor and safety reforms it forced.

title: Black Tom explosion rocks the harbor
book: Sabotage at Black Tom: Imperial Germany's Secret War in America, 1914-1917
author: Jules Witcover
note: The standard account of the German sabotage campaign that culminated in the massive Black Tom munitions explosion.

title: Immigration Act imposes national quotas
book: Impossible Subjects: Illegal Aliens and the Making of Modern America
author: Mae M. Ngai
note: The Bancroft- and Parkman-honored study of how the 1924 national-origins quotas remade American immigration and citizenship.

title: Harlem Renaissance blossoms
book: When Harlem Was in Vogue
author: David Levering Lewis
note: The classic, widely cited narrative history of the Harlem Renaissance by a two-time Pulitzer-winning historian.

title: Wall Street bombing kills 38
book: The Day Wall Street Exploded: A Story of America in Its First Age of Terror
author: Beverly Gage
note: The authoritative history of the 1920 Wall Street bombing set against America's first age of terrorism and radical politics.

title: Empire State Building opens
book: Higher: A Historic Race to the Sky and the Making of a City
author: Neal Bascomb
note: A narrative history of the late-1920s skyscraper race among the Chrysler, 40 Wall Street and Empire State towers.

title: Triborough Bridge opens
book: The Power Broker: Robert Moses and the Fall of New York
author: Robert A. Caro
note: The Pulitzer Prize-winning biography of Robert Moses, whose Triborough Authority financed his transformation of the city and region.

title: Fiorello La Guardia elected mayor
book: Fiorello La Guardia and the Making of Modern New York
author: Thomas Kessner
note: The leading scholarly biography of the reform mayor who ended Tammany's grip and rebuilt the city with the New Deal.

title: New York World's Fair opens in Queens
book: Dawn of a New Day: The New York World's Fair, 1939/40
author: Helen A. Harrison (ed.)
note: The catalogue-history from the Queens Museum's landmark exhibition on the 1939-40 fair and its 'World of Tomorrow' vision.
